title: Three‑electrode circuit element (point‑contact transistor, Bell Labs)
description: First commercial transistor; replaced bulky vacuum tubes and enabled modern electronics

title: Circuit element using semiconductor material (junction transistor)
description: Shockley’s junction transistor improved reliability and became the basis of discrete transistors

title: Electric‑field controlled semiconductor device (MOSFET)
description: Introduced the metal‑oxide‑semiconductor field‑effect transistor (MOSFET), the workhorse of integrated circuits

title: Miniature semiconductor integrated circuit (Jack Kilby/TI)
description: First monolithic integrated circuit; essential for microchips

title: Memory system for a multi‑chip digital computer
description: Allowed multiple chips to act as a microprocessor memory system—an early microprocessor patent

title: Field‑effect transistor memory (DRAM cell)
description: Robert Dennard’s dynamic RAM cell; made inexpensive high‑density memory possible

title: Self‑alignment method for integrated circuits (photolithography)
description: Introduced the silicon gate process used in MOS fabrication; improved speed and reliability

title: Light‑emitting diode (Gallium arsenide LED)
description: One of the first practical visible‑light LEDs; led to indicator lights and displays

title: Charge‑coupled device (CCD) imaging sensor
description: Invented the CCD sensor used in early digital cameras

title: Electrically programmable ROM (EPROM)
description: Dov Frohman’s EPROM allowed non‑volatile programmable memory

title: Magnetic bubble memory device
description: Provided rugged non‑volatile memory for early portable devices

title: Laser diode
description: Achieved continuous‑wave operation; foundation for optical communications

title: Fiber‑optic cable with graded‑index core
description: Enabled long‑distance high‑bandwidth optical communications

title: LZ78 (data compression)
description: One of the Lempel–Ziv patents used in data compression (GIF, TIFF)

title: CMOS image sensor
description: Early CMOS active‑pixel sensor enabling low‑power digital cameras

title: Lithium‑ion battery
description: Miyasaka’s patent for Li‑ion battery technology powering portable electronics

title: RISC microprocessor architecture
description: Early patent describing reduced‑instruction‑set computing; foundation for modern CPUs

title: Non‑volatile EEPROM memory
description: Described electrically erasable programmable ROM for BIOS and embedded systems

title: Floating‑point coprocessor
description: Added hardware floating‑point operations to microprocessors (Intel 8087)

title: LZW data compression (Unisys GIF patent)
description: Lempel–Ziv–Welch algorithm used in GIF images; widely licensed

title: (see previous)
description: Duplicate entry due to two patents with similar number; LZW algorithm patent also referenced as US4558302 (below)

title: RSA encryption algorithm
description: First public‑key cryptography system; basis for secure communications

title: 3D graphics rasterization pipeline
description: Provided hardware pipeline for real‑time 3D graphics; foundation for GPUs

title: JPEG image compression (Forgent Networks)
description: Covered discrete‑cosine transform techniques used in JPEG; later invalidated but historically important

title: EEPROM memory cell array
description: Improved endurance and erase characteristics for flash memory

title: Huffman coding (data compression)
description: Described adaptive Huffman coding used in communication and compression

title: GPS receiver (satellite navigation)
description: Early patent for portable GPS receivers

title: CMOS microcontroller architecture
description: Low‑power microcontrollers enabling embedded systems (e.g., Intel 8051 derivatives)

title: DRAM refresh circuitry
description: Automated refresh circuits for dynamic memory; improved reliability

title: Flash EPROM (flash memory)
description: Fujio Masuoka’s flash memory device; basis for USB drives and SSDs

title: Matrix multiplication accelerator
description: Hardware accelerator for matrix math used in signal processing and ML

title: LZW algorithm improvements (IBM patent)
description: Another LZW‑based compression patent; widely licensed for GIF

title: ARM processor architecture
description: Reduced‑instruction set microprocessor used in mobile devices

title: CMOS microprocessor integrated circuit with dual‑phase clock
description: Provided high‑speed clocking for microprocessors

title: Surface acoustic wave filter for cell phones
description: Enabled small filters used in mobile phones

title: MPEG audio compression (MP3)
description: One of the key MP3 patents used for digital audio encoding

title: CMOS logic gate for microprocessors
description: Allowed low‑power, high‑speed logic circuits

title: Lithium‑polymer battery
description: Provided safe, high‑energy rechargeable batteries for portable electronics

title: Mach microkernel (OS microkernel architecture)
description: Covered microkernel design used in Mac OS and other OSs

title: JPEG2000 wavelet compression
description: Patented wavelet techniques; later used in JPEG 2000 and GIS imaging

title: CMOS radio‑frequency transceiver
description: Miniaturized RF circuits enabling wireless modems

title: Wi‑Fi (IEEE 802.11) network
description: Described spread‑spectrum wireless LAN; basis for Wi‑Fi

title: Deep neural network training apparatus
description: Early neural network patent for training weights with back‑propagation

title: NAND flash memory structure
description: Provided the architecture used in modern solid‑state drives

title: JPEG motion picture compression (MPEG‑1 video)
description: Enabled digital video compression used in VCD and early DVD

title: Cellular baseband processor
description: Provided single‑chip baseband processing for digital cell phones

title: CMOS image sensor with pinned photodiode
description: Improved digital camera sensors; basis for smartphone cameras

title: USB interface controller
description: Provided a standard serial bus for connecting peripherals

title: SMP multicore computer architecture
description: Early symmetric multiprocessing design enabling dual‑core CPUs

title: Convolutional neural network image recognition
description: First CNN patent used for handwriting and object recognition

title: Secure Sockets Layer (SSL) encryption
description: Covered a method of secure data exchange over networks

title: Lithium‑ion cell with graphite anode
description: Improved safety and capacity of Li‑ion batteries

title: Flash file system (FAT for flash)
description: Provided robust file system for flash memory devices

title: MP3 audio encoding (Fraunhofer patent)
description: One of the MP3 patents used for digital music【620492278301110†L245-L257】

title: Synchronous DRAM (SDRAM)
description: Improved memory performance by synchronizing with CPU clock

title: USB data transfer protocol
description: Standardized communication protocol for USB devices

title: Java virtual machine (just‑in‑time compilation)
description: Covered dynamic translation of Java bytecode to native code

title: CDMA wireless system
description: Covered key aspects of Code Division Multiple Access cell‑phone technology

title: Multithreading processor architecture
description: Allowed multiple threads to execute concurrently on a single core

title: Diffie–Hellman key exchange implementation
description: Described practical method for secure key exchange

title: CMOS high‑density FPGA architecture
description: Provided reconfigurable logic devices used in prototyping and data centers

title: Amazon “1‑click” purchasing
description: Pioneered one‑click online purchases; influential in e‑commerce

title: Wi‑Fi (OFDM modulation)
description: Covered orthogonal frequency‑division multiplexing used in 802.11a/g

title: Browser plug‑in for embedded objects (Eolas)
description: Patent for automatically invoking external applications to display embedded objects; enforced against Microsoft

title: AES candidate encryption (Rijndael)
description: Early patent on the Rijndael encryption algorithm; basis for AES

title: Microarchitectural out‑of‑order execution
description: Provided technique for superscalar processors to reorder instructions

title: Amazon 1‑Click business method
description: Secondary Amazon patent for single‑action ordering

title: USB flash drive
description: Combined flash memory and USB connector into portable storage

title: Peer‑to‑peer network for file sharing
description: Early patent describing distributed peer‑to‑peer file sharing (Napster‑like)

title: Multi‑touch input surface
description: Precursor to later multi‑touch patents; allowed sensing of multiple contacts

title: Bluetooth wireless system
description: Early patent covering Bluetooth network architecture

title: Self‑balancing personal transporter (Segway)
description: Described dynamic stabilization control for two‑wheeled transporters

title: AES encryption hardware implementation
description: Provided efficient hardware implementation of AES

title: Processor virtualization (hypervisor)
description: Covered virtualization layer enabling multiple OSs on one machine

title: Voice over Internet Protocol (VoIP)
description: Provided method for transmitting voice over IP networks

title: Brain‑computer interface (Utah Array)
description: Implantable electrode array for interfacing with the brain; used in neural prosthetics

title: Retinal prosthesis (bionic eye)
description: Implantable retinal prosthesis restoring partial vision

title: Peer‑to‑peer information exchange (Bluetooth improvements)
description: Further Bluetooth patent covering information exchange for mobile devices

title: PageRank algorithm (Google)
description: Method for ranking nodes in a linked database; foundation of Google search

title: Virtual reality generator (VR headset)
description: Patent describing VR environment for financial data; early VR headset

title: Scalable parallel supercomputer architecture
description: Provided high‑performance computing design used in clusters

title: GPU programmable shading
description: Introduced programmable graphics pipelines used in modern GPUs

title: Wi‑Fi security (WPA)
description: Described temporal key integrity protocol used for wireless security

title: NAND flash controller for solid‑state disks
description: Controller architecture enabling SSDs and USB drives

title: GPU multi‑threaded architecture (NVIDIA)
description: Allowed GPUs to execute many threads simultaneously; basis for CUDA

title: Streaming media distribution
description: Method for streaming audio/video over the internet; foundation for Netflix and YouTube

title: MapReduce data processing
description: Early patent on distributed data processing used in Hadoop and big data

title: SIFT (scale‑invariant feature transform)
description: Computer‑vision technique for identifying features in images; used in object recognition

title: AES cryptographic hardware
description: Efficient hardware implementation for AES encryption

title: Drone quadcopter (vertical‑lift helicopter drone)
description: Patent for an omni‑directional vertical‑lift drone; basis for modern quadcopters

title: Cryptographic hash function (SHA‑3 candidate)
description: Described a secure hash function used in digital signatures

title: 3D printing via stereolithography
description: Patent for apparatus for producing three‑dimensional objects by stereolithography

description: Patent describing production of nano‑scaled graphene plates; early graphene fabrication

title: Solar cell with energy storage
description: Improved photovoltaic cell with integrated energy storage

description: Viola‑Jones face detection algorithm using cascaded classifiers

description: Patent describing CRISPR‑Cas systems for altering gene expression

description: Combined FPGA fabric with embedded processor; used in SoCs

title: Multi‑touch user interface (Apple)
description: Apple patent covering multi‑touch input for portable devices

description: Touch gesture for unlocking a device; widely used on smartphones

description: VisLab’s patent for autonomous vehicle vision and sensor fusion

description: Describes a system for brain implants to control external devices

description: Patent covering ad‑hoc peer‑to‑peer networking (Bluetooth related)
